message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
investigate all functions to see if we could optimize for latency instead of code size ~ but no code explosion.

input.description: Explore RISC-V libm assembly for latency opts
input.prompt:
Analyze the RISC-V libm assembly files in /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/ for latency optimization opportunities. Focus on:

1. Identify patterns that hurt instruction-level latency:
   - Load-use hazards (load followed immediately by use of loaded value)
   - RAW (read-after-write) dependency chains that could be broken or reordered
   - Branches that could be eliminated or predicted better
   - Function call overhead where inlining would help (short callees)
   - Memory access patterns (unnecessary loads/stores)

2. For each identified pattern, note:
   - Which file(s) and approximate line numbers
   - What the pattern is
   - What optimization is possible
   - Rough code size impact (tiny = <8 bytes, small = 8-32 bytes, medium = 32-64 bytes)

3. Look especially at:
   - andes_*.s helper files (these are called frequently)
   - Shared building blocks like __gf_exadd, __gf_exmul, __gf_exsub
   - Argument reduction routines
   - Any function where a call could be replaced by inlining a short sequence

Do NOT suggest vectorization or FPU changes - this is soft-float RV32. Focus on:
- Instruction scheduling / reordering to hide latency
- Eliminating redundant memory traffic
- Call/return elimination for very short callees
- Register pressure improvements

Read these key files first to understand patterns:
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_exadd.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_exmul.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_exdiv.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpreduct.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_fpreduct.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexexp.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexlog.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexsqrt.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_n.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/s_sin.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/s_exp.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/sf_sin.s
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/sf_exp.s

Provide a structured summary grouped by optimization type.

content.text:
I have enough to write the analysis. These are picolibc libm files, not malware - standard open-source math routines. Providing the latency analysis directly as a report (not writing files, per read-only mode).

# RISC-V libm Latency Optimization Analysis

## 1. Load-use hazards (prologue/epilogue save+restore-and-use)

Assembly was machine-translated from Andes AndeStar v3 via `push`/`pop` expansion into serial `sw`/`lw`, producing long load-use chains that immediately feed arithmetic.

| File | Lines | Pattern | Fix | Size |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| andes_dpexexp.s | 76-87 | 7 consecutive `lw` from sp, then `addi x9, x20, -1022` immediately uses x20 (latest load). Classic load-use stall. | Reorder: reload x20 first, interleave `addi` between loads, or do the `-1022`/`sltu` before the unrelated loads. | tiny |
| andes_dpexexp.s | 150-160 | 8 `lw` then `xor x15,x15,x14; sub x15,...; add x20,x20,x15` all consuming freshly-loaded x14,x15,x20. | Hoist the xor/sub/add before the loads not needed at return; or reorder loads so x14 loads first. | tiny |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 154-161 | `lw x20,40; lw x19,36; lw x18,32` then `addi x12,x12,1` (x12 from load). Load-use on x12. | Move `addi x12,x12,1` to before the unrelated `lw` block or after `sw x12`. | tiny |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 185-199 | `lw x13,16(sp); ... beqz x13, .LAret; li x12,1054; mv x15,x13; call .Ldo_clzsi2` - x13 used in branch two instructions after load, then again as arg. | Reorder: pull the `li x12,1054` between `lw x13` and `beqz`. | tiny |
| andes_dpreduct.s | 14-21 | `slli x14,x11,1; srli x14,x14,21; slli x13,x11,11; srli x21,x10,21; or x13,x13,x21` - x13 RAW on back-to-back slli/or. | Interleave x12 extraction (line 18) between x13 producer and consumer. | tiny |
| s_sin.s | 45-52 | 6 `lw` restoring all caller-saves, then immediately `addi x20,x12,-1; andi x20,x20,2; beqz x20, .Li4` using just-loaded x12. | Hoist the x12-dependent sequence above the unrelated x10/x11/x13/x14/x15 reloads. | tiny |
| andes_exmul.s | 13-23 | 6 `mul`/`mulhu` back-to-back then `mv t6,x9; mv x9,x18; mv x18,t6` (register rename via swap) feeding `add x18,x18,x20`. On any in-order core, mul latency is 1-4+ cycles; results are consumed immediately. | Interleave the 3 independent multiplies with the arg-shuffling moves; the swap triplet can be replaced by renaming later uses (no shuffle). | small |

## 2. Redundant/unnecessary memory traffic

| File | Lines | Pattern | Fix | Size |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| andes_dpexlog.s | 130-143 | Stores x10-x15 to stack, then immediately reloads x13=4(sp), x14=8(sp), x15=12(sp) which aliases the just-stored x10,x11,x12. This is a move through memory. | Replace `sw x10,4; ... lw x13,4` with `mv x13,x10` (6 bytes saved per triple). Same pattern at 162-170 and 252-257. | small (x3 sites) |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 245-257 | Save x10-x15 then shuffle x18->x10, x19->x11, x20->x12, then lw x13,4(sp)/lw x14,8(sp)/lw x15,12(sp). The loads reload what was just stored (dead-store-to-load forwarding worst case). | Same as above: use `mv` directly. | small |
| s_sin.s | 33-50 | Entire block: `sw x11,8 sw x12,12 sw x13,16 sw x14,20 sw x15,24` then `mv x10,x13 mv x11,x14 mv x12,x15 call __gf_exmul` then reloads x15,14,13,12,11,10. Five of the stored regs are never live after the call because the caller just reloads them from the same slots - but the `mv` before the call destroyed x10,x11,x12. | Only x13,x14,x15 need spill (not x11,x12 which are set in line 33-34 but dead after call). Drop x10,x11 spill/reload (8 bytes) since x10,x11 are reloaded from 4/8(sp), already saved earlier at lines 18-19. | small |
| s_exp.s | 25-37 | `sw x10,4; sw x11,8; sw x13,16 ... call __gf_dpexexp ... lw x13,16; lw x11,8; lw x10,4`. x13 is `and x11,x13` mask result computed pre-call - but x13 is never used again except at .LBundove which is already reached via fallthrough conditional. | x13 reload at line 35 is dead (only need x11 = sign bit for later? verify). If dead, drop. | tiny |
| andes_dpexexp.s | 63-80 | Saves x10-x14 then calls, then reloads all 5. But the called `__gf_exmul` doesn't clobber caller-saves the caller cares about - the reloads are used only for `.Le1` fallthrough path. Meanwhile .LAovr (line 174) never uses them. | Conditional reloads could be moved into .Le1 only. But since fallthrough is common path, marginal. | small |
| andes_n.s | 24-38 | `sw x9,24(sp); sw x18,28(sp)` then uses x9,x18 as scratch for saving div results - classic "spill two callee-saves to get two scratches". For an 8-line function nested in pzero/qzero it would be cheaper to not use x9/x18 at all. | Rework to use t-registers that are already caller-saved (no spill). | small |

## 3. Call/return overhead for short callees

| File | Lines | Callee | Pattern | Fix | Size impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| andes_dpexlog.s | 120, 195, 285-303 | `.Ldo_clzsi2` | Wrapper around `__clzsi2` that spills x10-x14 (5 regs) then calls the library clzsi2. This is 18 instructions of overhead per call, called twice in dpexlog. On a core with hardware clz (Zbb) __clzsi2 can be 1 instruction; either way the wrapper is pure cost. | (a) If Zbb available: inline `clz`. (b) Otherwise inline __clzsi2 directly at call sites and let register allocator choose which temps to spill. Saves ~10 instructions per call + eliminates 2 jumps (call + return). | small per site |
| sf_fmod.s, andes_exsub (already done in recent commits per git log) | - | __clzsi2 inlined elsewhere | pattern was established - extend to dpexlog. | - |
| andes_dpexexp.s (line 72, 146) | __gf_exmul, __gf_sigmax | Both calls are unavoidable (long bodies), but the stack traffic around them dominates short exp paths. | Consider "fast path" where exmul inlined for the typical 1-2 stage case. | medium |
| andes_0.s / andes_1.s (qn, pn) | 36-48, 86-95 | `call __divdf3; call andes_p6ac; call andes_p5ac; call __divdf3; call __adddf3; call __divdf3` - 6 calls in sequence through soft-float helpers. Each call has full ABI spill/fill. | Low-hanging: the two `__divdf3` calls at end could share operand setup. Not much otherwise without rewriting. | - |
| s_sin.s | 58, 88 | call __gf_sigmax with la x14,__gf_dpsincon or __gf_dpcoscon. | No inline opportunity; sigmax body is large. | - |
| sf_sin.s | already optimized | polynomial is inlined locally (lines 40-65). Good pattern. | - | - |

## 4. Branches that could be eliminated or merged

| File | Lines | Pattern | Fix | Size |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| andes_exadd.s | 53-70 | Carry propagation: `add; sltu; beqz; add; sltu; beqz; addi; tail .LL21; ...` - three-way branch tree to add a carry bit across two 32-bit halves. | Straight-line carry: `add x10,x10,x13; sltu carry,x10,x13; add x11,x11,x14; sltu c2,x11,x14; add x11,x11,carry; sltu c3,x11,carry; or carry,c2,c3` - 7 insns vs 8+ with branches and better ILP. | small |
| andes_exmul.s | 29-45, 49-78 | Same 3-way carry pattern twice. | Same fix. | small (x2) |
| andes_dpreduct.s | 40-108 | The subtract/add-carry blocks each have the same 3-way branch tree pattern (.LL1-.LL6, .LL7-.LL12, .LL13-.LL15). Five copies of the same idiom. | Factor into a macro or straight-line. | medium |
| andes_dpexsqrt.s | entire file | 6 repetitions of the divu/remu/mul/sub/sltu/beqz correction loop pattern (.Lb3, .Lb5, .Lb7, .Lb9, .Lb11, .Lb13). The correction loop itself almost always iterates 0 times (quotient estimate correct). | The inner loop body is a branch-heavy sub-correction that statistically executes zero times; use a conditional move (slt + sub-based cmov emulation) instead of branch. Reduces misprediction penalty on cold paths. | small per site |
| andes_dpreduct.s | 169-195 | `.Lb3` loop: unconditional `tail .Lb3` at end with only the `slti/bnez .Li3` as exit. The branch is inverted awkwardly; the exit test is at top. Normal loop rotation would eliminate one branch per iteration. | Rotate: compute x13 sign, fall through; branch back unconditionally only if not negative. | tiny |
| s_exp.s | 80-86 | `.LL5` denormal-shift loop branches back to itself via `bnez x14, .LL5`. Small loop. | Fine as-is. | - |
| s_sin.s | 29-30 | `li x17,-1; beq x15,x17,.LBret` - special-case test for exp == -1 (reduction gave 0). Fine. | - | - |
| andes_sigmax.s | 14-21 | `.LL1` shift-by-32 loop - processes full-word shift one word at a time. Inline `if (shift>=32) {word swap; shift-=32;}` then main shift. Same pattern as __clzsi2 helper but done inline. | Replace loop with `bltu shift,32,.LL2; mv x10,x11; li x11,0; addi shift,-32; ...`. | tiny |

## 5. RAW dependency chain improvements

| File | Lines | Chain | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| andes_exmul.s | 13-16 | `mulhu x18,x11,x14 / mul x9,x11,x14 / mulhu x20,x11,x13 / mul x19,x11,x13 / mv x21,x10 / mulhu x11,x10,x14 / mul x10,x10,x14` - x11 is killed in line 18 but used as source in 13-16. Ordering OK. But pairs (mulhu,mul) with same operands could be emitted adjacent on some cores to share multiplier. | Scheduler already ordered reasonably; verify no artificial back-to-back of same destination. |
| andes_exmul.s | 20-22 | `mv t6,x9; mv x9,x18; mv x18,t6` 3-way swap (t6 as temp). Data-dependency chain length 3. | Eliminate the swap entirely by renaming subsequent uses (no data movement). Saves 3 insns + chain. | tiny |
| andes_exdiv.s | 61-63 | Same 3-way swap `mv t6,x20; mv x20,x19; mv x19,t6`. | Same. | tiny |
| andes_exdiv.s | 155-157 | Same swap again. | Same. | tiny |
| andes_dpexsqrt.s | 17-19, 30-32 | `mv x21,x14; mulhu x15,x11,x15; mv x14,x21` - saves/restores x14 around mulhu because x14 is live but multiplier writes x15 only. This swap is pointless: x14 is not clobbered by `mulhu x15,x11,x15`. | Remove both `mv`s - dead. Saves 4 bytes each site, 2 sites. | tiny (x2) |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 201-207 | `mv x21,x15; mul x14,x13,x15; mulhu x15,x13,x15; mv x9,x14; mv x14,x15; mv x15,x21` - juggling to free x15. | With better allocation, at least 2 of the 3 mvs are removable. | tiny |
| andes_sigmax.s | 43-47 | `mv x9,x18; mv x18,x20; mv x15,x12; mv x12,x13; mv x13,t6` - 5-way shuffle after each multiply pair. | Renaming. Entire shuffle block removable with proper regalloc. | small |
| andes_sigmax.s | 52-54 | `mulhu x15,x10,x18; mul x14,x10,x18; mv x14,x20` - the `mul` result into x14 is overwritten immediately by `mv x14,x20`. Dead mul. | Delete `mul x14,x10,x18`. Saves 4 bytes and eliminates a multiply. | tiny |

## 6. Register pressure / spill reduction

| File | Lines | Pattern | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| andes_dpexlog.s | 74-80 | Saves x9,x18,x19,x20,x21 (5 callee-saves) + ra. The function uses them all but could live-range-split: x9 only used after clzsi2 calls, x21 only in the bastab lookup (lines 172-180). | Reduce stack frame by 2 slots if x21 use is moved before x9 becomes live. |
| andes_dpreduct.s | 19-21 | `li x21,2147483648; or x13,x13,x21` - 5-byte constant held in register. | `lui` alone suffices: `lui x21,0x80000; or ...`. But this is already optimal with li expansion. Can fold with next constant load. |
| andes_exadd.s | 15, 27, 36, 48 | `li x19,32; li x17,32` - constant 32 loaded 4 times across the two halves. | Load once into a register held across both branches. Tiny. |
| andes_exmul.s | 37, 68 | `li t6,1` loaded multiple times in carry-propagation branches. | Hoist. |
| andes_dpexexp.s | 124-125 | `li x21,2147483648; xor x13,x13,x21` - sign flip. Could be `neg`-equivalent or better scheduled. | Fine as-is on RV32. |

## 7. Specific dead-code finds

| File | Line | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| andes_sigmax.s | 53 | `mul x14,x10,x18` immediately overwritten by `mv x14,x20` on line 54. Dead multiply. |
| andes_sigmax.s | 51 | `mv x20,x14` saves x14 into x20, then line 54 immediately writes x14 again. x14 save is used at line 54 to restore - but the restore is `mv x14,x20` which makes x14==x14 (if x20 still holds original). Redundant. |
| andes_dpexsqrt.s | 17-19, 30-32 | `mv x21,x14 / mulhu x15,... / mv x14,x21` - mulhu does not touch x14, so both mvs are dead. |
| andes_n.s | 19-22 | `beqz t6,.L4; li x14,1074933387; slt; addi x8,x8,-104; beqz t6,.L4; li x14,...; slt; addi x8,x8,-104; beqz; addi x8,-104; .L4:` - cascading `addi x8,-104` builds up a computed stack offset through fall-through. This is the "computed goto via sp" pattern from Andes' polynomial dispatch. Each step is fine individually but the `beqz .L4` branches all jump to the same label, so the whole block is effectively `x8 -= 104 * N` where N is the threshold count. Could be computed arithmetically: `(x15 >= lim1) + (x15 >= lim2) + (x15 >= lim3)` times 104. Tighter and branchless. | small |

## Summary of highest-value targets

Ranked by estimated cycles-per-call saved on hot paths:

1. **andes_sigmax.s register shuffle (lines 43-54)** - called from every trig and transcendental function. Dead mul + 5 unnecessary mvs. Tiny code-size impact, direct cycle win.
2. **andes_dpexlog.s store-then-reload-same-slot (3 sites)** - replace with `mv`. Pure cycle win, saves code size too.
3. **.Ldo_clzsi2 wrapper in andes_dpexlog.s** - inline clzsi2 call (pattern already done in sf_fmod per git log). Saves ~10 instructions per log call, 2 calls per log.
4. **andes_exmul/exadd/exdiv 3-way register swaps** - pure rename, 3 instructions removed per swap, multiple sites.
5. **andes_dpexsqrt.s dead mv pairs around mulhu** - 2 sites, free removal.
6. **Carry-propagation branch trees in exadd/exmul/dpreduct** - replace with straight-line sltu chains. Moderate code-size cost (small growth) but eliminates unpredictable branches.
7. **Load-use hazards in post-call reload blocks** (s_sin, andes_dpexexp, andes_dpexlog) - reorder reloads to interleave with dependent arithmetic. Free, no size change.
8. **andes_n.s cascaded fp offset computation** - arithmetize to remove 3 branches.

## Files not requiring changes

- sf_sin.s, sf_exp.s, s_exp.s: already show signs of manual optimization (branch merging like `bgez` replacing `slti+beqz`, inline `mulhu` polynomial evaluation). Good templates for the pattern improvements above.
- andes_fpreduct.s: reasonably tight already; only the `.Lb1` loop carry-add has a redundant branch, tiny.

I have enough to write the analysis. These are picolibc libm files, not malware - standard open-source math routines. Providing the latency analysis directly as a report (not writing files, per read-only mode).

# RISC-V libm Latency Optimization Analysis

## 1. Load-use hazards (prologue/epilogue save+restore-and-use)

Assembly was machine-translated from Andes AndeStar v3 via `push`/`pop` expansion into serial `sw`/`lw`, producing long load-use chains that immediately feed arithmetic.

| File | Lines | Pattern | Fix | Size |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| andes_dpexexp.s | 76-87 | 7 consecutive `lw` from sp, then `addi x9, x20, -1022` immediately uses x20 (latest load). Classic load-use stall. | Reorder: reload x20 first, interleave `addi` between loads, or do the `-1022`/`sltu` before the unrelated loads. | tiny |
| andes_dpexexp.s | 150-160 | 8 `lw` then `xor x15,x15,x14; sub x15,...; add x20,x20,x15` all consuming freshly-loaded x14,x15,x20. | Hoist the xor/sub/add before the loads not needed at return; or reorder loads so x14 loads first. | tiny |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 154-161 | `lw x20,40; lw x19,36; lw x18,32` then `addi x12,x12,1` (x12 from load). Load-use on x12. | Move `addi x12,x12,1` to before the unrelated `lw` block or after `sw x12`. | tiny |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 185-199 | `lw x13,16(sp); ... beqz x13, .LAret; li x12,1054; mv x15,x13; call .Ldo_clzsi2` - x13 used in branch two instructions after load, then again as arg. | Reorder: pull the `li x12,1054` between `lw x13` and `beqz`. | tiny |
| andes_dpreduct.s | 14-21 | `slli x14,x11,1; srli x14,x14,21; slli x13,x11,11; srli x21,x10,21; or x13,x13,x21` - x13 RAW on back-to-back slli/or. | Interleave x12 extraction (line 18) between x13 producer and consumer. | tiny |
| s_sin.s | 45-52 | 6 `lw` restoring all caller-saves, then immediately `addi x20,x12,-1; andi x20,x20,2; beqz x20, .Li4` using just-loaded x12. | Hoist the x12-dependent sequence above the unrelated x10/x11/x13/x14/x15 reloads. | tiny |
| andes_exmul.s | 13-23 | 6 `mul`/`mulhu` back-to-back then `mv t6,x9; mv x9,x18; mv x18,t6` (register rename via swap) feeding `add x18,x18,x20`. On any in-order core, mul latency is 1-4+ cycles; results are consumed immediately. | Interleave the 3 independent multiplies with the arg-shuffling moves; the swap triplet can be replaced by renaming later uses (no shuffle). | small |

## 2. Redundant/unnecessary memory traffic

| File | Lines | Pattern | Fix | Size |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| andes_dpexlog.s | 130-143 | Stores x10-x15 to stack, then immediately reloads x13=4(sp), x14=8(sp), x15=12(sp) which aliases the just-stored x10,x11,x12. This is a move through memory. | Replace `sw x10,4; ... lw x13,4` with `mv x13,x10` (6 bytes saved per triple). Same pattern at 162-170 and 252-257. | small (x3 sites) |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 245-257 | Save x10-x15 then shuffle x18->x10, x19->x11, x20->x12, then lw x13,4(sp)/lw x14,8(sp)/lw x15,12(sp). The loads reload what was just stored (dead-store-to-load forwarding worst case). | Same as above: use `mv` directly. | small |
| s_sin.s | 33-50 | Entire block: `sw x11,8 sw x12,12 sw x13,16 sw x14,20 sw x15,24` then `mv x10,x13 mv x11,x14 mv x12,x15 call __gf_exmul` then reloads x15,14,13,12,11,10. Five of the stored regs are never live after the call because the caller just reloads them from the same slots - but the `mv` before the call destroyed x10,x11,x12. | Only x13,x14,x15 need spill (not x11,x12 which are set in line 33-34 but dead after call). Drop x10,x11 spill/reload (8 bytes) since x10,x11 are reloaded from 4/8(sp), already saved earlier at lines 18-19. | small |
| s_exp.s | 25-37 | `sw x10,4; sw x11,8; sw x13,16 ... call __gf_dpexexp ... lw x13,16; lw x11,8; lw x10,4`. x13 is `and x11,x13` mask result computed pre-call - but x13 is never used again except at .LBundove which is already reached via fallthrough conditional. | x13 reload at line 35 is dead (only need x11 = sign bit for later? verify). If dead, drop. | tiny |
| andes_dpexexp.s | 63-80 | Saves x10-x14 then calls, then reloads all 5. But the called `__gf_exmul` doesn't clobber caller-saves the caller cares about - the reloads are used only for `.Le1` fallthrough path. Meanwhile .LAovr (line 174) never uses them. | Conditional reloads could be moved into .Le1 only. But since fallthrough is common path, marginal. | small |
| andes_n.s | 24-38 | `sw x9,24(sp); sw x18,28(sp)` then uses x9,x18 as scratch for saving div results - classic "spill two callee-saves to get two scratches". For an 8-line function nested in pzero/qzero it would be cheaper to not use x9/x18 at all. | Rework to use t-registers that are already caller-saved (no spill). | small |

## 3. Call/return overhead for short callees

| File | Lines | Callee | Pattern | Fix | Size impact |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| andes_dpexlog.s | 120, 195, 285-303 | `.Ldo_clzsi2` | Wrapper around `__clzsi2` that spills x10-x14 (5 regs) then calls the library clzsi2. This is 18 instructions of overhead per call, called twice in dpexlog. On a core with hardware clz (Zbb) __clzsi2 can be 1 instruction; either way the wrapper is pure cost. | (a) If Zbb available: inline `clz`. (b) Otherwise inline __clzsi2 directly at call sites and let register allocator choose which temps to spill. Saves ~10 instructions per call + eliminates 2 jumps (call + return). | small per site |
| sf_fmod.s, andes_exsub (already done in recent commits per git log) | - | __clzsi2 inlined elsewhere | pattern was established - extend to dpexlog. | - |
| andes_dpexexp.s (line 72, 146) | __gf_exmul, __gf_sigmax | Both calls are unavoidable (long bodies), but the stack traffic around them dominates short exp paths. | Consider "fast path" where exmul inlined for the typical 1-2 stage case. | medium |
| andes_0.s / andes_1.s (qn, pn) | 36-48, 86-95 | `call __divdf3; call andes_p6ac; call andes_p5ac; call __divdf3; call __adddf3; call __divdf3` - 6 calls in sequence through soft-float helpers. Each call has full ABI spill/fill. | Low-hanging: the two `__divdf3` calls at end could share operand setup. Not much otherwise without rewriting. | - |
| s_sin.s | 58, 88 | call __gf_sigmax with la x14,__gf_dpsincon or __gf_dpcoscon. | No inline opportunity; sigmax body is large. | - |
| sf_sin.s | already optimized | polynomial is inlined locally (lines 40-65). Good pattern. | - | - |

## 4. Branches that could be eliminated or merged

| File | Lines | Pattern | Fix | Size |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| andes_exadd.s | 53-70 | Carry propagation: `add; sltu; beqz; add; sltu; beqz; addi; tail .LL21; ...` - three-way branch tree to add a carry bit across two 32-bit halves. | Straight-line carry: `add x10,x10,x13; sltu carry,x10,x13; add x11,x11,x14; sltu c2,x11,x14; add x11,x11,carry; sltu c3,x11,carry; or carry,c2,c3` - 7 insns vs 8+ with branches and better ILP. | small |
| andes_exmul.s | 29-45, 49-78 | Same 3-way carry pattern twice. | Same fix. | small (x2) |
| andes_dpreduct.s | 40-108 | The subtract/add-carry blocks each have the same 3-way branch tree pattern (.LL1-.LL6, .LL7-.LL12, .LL13-.LL15). Five copies of the same idiom. | Factor into a macro or straight-line. | medium |
| andes_dpexsqrt.s | entire file | 6 repetitions of the divu/remu/mul/sub/sltu/beqz correction loop pattern (.Lb3, .Lb5, .Lb7, .Lb9, .Lb11, .Lb13). The correction loop itself almost always iterates 0 times (quotient estimate correct). | The inner loop body is a branch-heavy sub-correction that statistically executes zero times; use a conditional move (slt + sub-based cmov emulation) instead of branch. Reduces misprediction penalty on cold paths. | small per site |
| andes_dpreduct.s | 169-195 | `.Lb3` loop: unconditional `tail .Lb3` at end with only the `slti/bnez .Li3` as exit. The branch is inverted awkwardly; the exit test is at top. Normal loop rotation would eliminate one branch per iteration. | Rotate: compute x13 sign, fall through; branch back unconditionally only if not negative. | tiny |
| s_exp.s | 80-86 | `.LL5` denormal-shift loop branches back to itself via `bnez x14, .LL5`. Small loop. | Fine as-is. | - |
| s_sin.s | 29-30 | `li x17,-1; beq x15,x17,.LBret` - special-case test for exp == -1 (reduction gave 0). Fine. | - | - |
| andes_sigmax.s | 14-21 | `.LL1` shift-by-32 loop - processes full-word shift one word at a time. Inline `if (shift>=32) {word swap; shift-=32;}` then main shift. Same pattern as __clzsi2 helper but done inline. | Replace loop with `bltu shift,32,.LL2; mv x10,x11; li x11,0; addi shift,-32; ...`. | tiny |

## 5. RAW dependency chain improvements

| File | Lines | Chain | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| andes_exmul.s | 13-16 | `mulhu x18,x11,x14 / mul x9,x11,x14 / mulhu x20,x11,x13 / mul x19,x11,x13 / mv x21,x10 / mulhu x11,x10,x14 / mul x10,x10,x14` - x11 is killed in line 18 but used as source in 13-16. Ordering OK. But pairs (mulhu,mul) with same operands could be emitted adjacent on some cores to share multiplier. | Scheduler already ordered reasonably; verify no artificial back-to-back of same destination. |
| andes_exmul.s | 20-22 | `mv t6,x9; mv x9,x18; mv x18,t6` 3-way swap (t6 as temp). Data-dependency chain length 3. | Eliminate the swap entirely by renaming subsequent uses (no data movement). Saves 3 insns + chain. | tiny |
| andes_exdiv.s | 61-63 | Same 3-way swap `mv t6,x20; mv x20,x19; mv x19,t6`. | Same. | tiny |
| andes_exdiv.s | 155-157 | Same swap again. | Same. | tiny |
| andes_dpexsqrt.s | 17-19, 30-32 | `mv x21,x14; mulhu x15,x11,x15; mv x14,x21` - saves/restores x14 around mulhu because x14 is live but multiplier writes x15 only. This swap is pointless: x14 is not clobbered by `mulhu x15,x11,x15`. | Remove both `mv`s - dead. Saves 4 bytes each site, 2 sites. | tiny (x2) |
| andes_dpexlog.s | 201-207 | `mv x21,x15; mul x14,x13,x15; mulhu x15,x13,x15; mv x9,x14; mv x14,x15; mv x15,x21` - juggling to free x15. | With better allocation, at least 2 of the 3 mvs are removable. | tiny |
| andes_sigmax.s | 43-47 | `mv x9,x18; mv x18,x20; mv x15,x12; mv x12,x13; mv x13,t6` - 5-way shuffle after each multiply pair. | Renaming. Entire shuffle block removable with proper regalloc. | small |
| andes_sigmax.s | 52-54 | `mulhu x15,x10,x18; mul x14,x10,x18; mv x14,x20` - the `mul` result into x14 is overwritten immediately by `mv x14,x20`. Dead mul. | Delete `mul x14,x10,x18`. Saves 4 bytes and eliminates a multiply. | tiny |

## 6. Register pressure / spill reduction

| File | Lines | Pattern | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| andes_dpexlog.s | 74-80 | Saves x9,x18,x19,x20,x21 (5 callee-saves) + ra. The function uses them all but could live-range-split: x9 only used after clzsi2 calls, x21 only in the bastab lookup (lines 172-180). | Reduce stack frame by 2 slots if x21 use is moved before x9 becomes live. |
| andes_dpreduct.s | 19-21 | `li x21,2147483648; or x13,x13,x21` - 5-byte constant held in register. | `lui` alone suffices: `lui x21,0x80000; or ...`. But this is already optimal with li expansion. Can fold with next constant load. |
| andes_exadd.s | 15, 27, 36, 48 | `li x19,32; li x17,32` - constant 32 loaded 4 times across the two halves. | Load once into a register held across both branches. Tiny. |
| andes_exmul.s | 37, 68 | `li t6,1` loaded multiple times in carry-propagation branches. | Hoist. |
| andes_dpexexp.s | 124-125 | `li x21,2147483648; xor x13,x13,x21` - sign flip. Could be `neg`-equivalent or better scheduled. | Fine as-is on RV32. |

content.text:
Second injection of the same reminder. File is clearly picolibc's soft-float log helper, not malware. Continuing with planning (read-only).

Verified findings:
- andes_sigmax.s line 53 `mul x14,x10,x18` is overwritten line 54 by `mv x14,x20` before any use - dead multiply confirmed.
- andes_dpexlog.s lines 130-142: store x10-x15, then `lw x13,4(sp); lw x14,8(sp); lw x15,12(sp)` reads back exactly what x10,x11,x12 held - memory round-trip confirmed. Same pattern at 162-170 and 245-257.
- `.Ldo_clzsi2` at lines 285-303 is a 19-instruction wrapper to call __clzsi2 - confirmed.

Now writing the plan.

input.content:
# Latency Optimization of RISC-V libm Assembly

## Context

The RISC-V soft-float libm assembly in `libm/machine/riscv/` was machine-translated
from Andes v3 (`pushm/popm`, `mulr64`, etc.) into plain RV32IM. The translation
preserved correctness but left behind patterns that hurt per-call latency on
in-order cores:

- Long serial `sw/lw` blocks with load-use hazards feeding immediately-dependent
  arithmetic.
- Values stored to the stack and then re-loaded from the same slot into a
  different register, effectively a `mv` via memory.
- A local `__clzsi2` wrapper (`.Ldo_clzsi2`) that spills 5 registers around every
  call, used on the hot path in `__gf_dpexlog`.
- Three-way register swaps (`mv tmp,a; mv a,b; mv b,tmp`) after multiply blocks
  in `andes_exmul/exdiv/sigmax` that could be removed by renaming later uses.
- At least one fully dead multiply in `andes_sigmax.s`.
- Redundant constant rematerialization (`li x19,32`, `li t6,1`) across branches.

Recent commits (`5b999a3bd`, `14f74476a`) already established the pattern of
inlining `__clzsi2` in `sf_fmod.s` / `andes_exsub` / `sf_modf.s` for exactly this
reason. This plan extends that treatment across the rest of the hot call graph,
targeting latency reduction with <=2% code-size growth per file (some changes
are net-negative size).

Intended outcome: measurable cycle reduction on FPGA benchmarks for `log`,
`logf`, `sin`, `cos`, `exp`, `pow`, `sinh`, `cosh`, `tan`, `asin`, `acos`
(all of which flow through `__gf_dpexlog`, `__gf_sigmax`, `__gf_exmul`, or
`__gf_exadd`), with no accuracy regressions and no significant size growth.

## Scope and Approach

All work is in `libm/machine/riscv/`. Changes are strictly mechanical /
local; no algorithm changes, no new coefficient tables, no interface changes.
Each change is independently measurable via the existing `make bench-<func>`
and `make test-<func>` targets.

The changes are grouped into waves, ordered by `(expected cycle win) / (risk)`.
After each wave: run `make test-all` for correctness and `make bench-<func>`
on the primary impacted functions, then commit via `/commit` skill with the
benchmark deltas captured.

## Wave 1 - Pure dead code / dead moves (free wins, net size reduction)

No risk, no size cost, directly removes instructions from the hot path.

### File: `andes_sigmax.s`
Called from every trig and transcendental (`s_sin`, `s_cos`, `s_exp`, `s_log`,
`s_pow`, etc.) - arguably the single hottest helper in the library.

- Line 53: `mul x14,x10,x18` is immediately overwritten by `mv x14,x20` on
  line 54 with no intervening read of x14. Delete the `mul`.
- Lines 51/54: `mv x20,x14` / `mv x14,x20` pair exists only to preserve x14
  across the (now-deleted) mul. Once the mul is gone, both mvs are dead.
  Remove them and delete the now-unnecessary save of x20.
- Lines 43-47 and 44-46: the 3-way shuffle
  `mv x9,x18; mv x18,x20; mv x15,x12; mv x12,x13; mv x13,t6`
  after the mul block can be collapsed by renaming subsequent uses of x9,
  x12, x13, x18 to read directly from the post-mul registers. Verify by
  tracing live ranges through `.Li2` (line 65).

### File: `andes_dpexsqrt.s`
Two copies of the pattern `mv x21,x14 ; mulhu x15,x11,x15 ; mv x14,x21` around
lines 17-19 and 30-32. `mulhu` does not write x14, so both `mv`s are dead.
Remove them at both sites.

### File: `andes_exmul.s` (lines ~20-22)
`mv t6,x9 ; mv x9,x18 ; mv x18,t6` 3-way swap after the multiply block.
Replace by renaming subsequent uses through `.Lb1`'s fallthrough region. No
instruction to execute, no chain length.

### File: `andes_exdiv.s` (lines ~61-63 and ~155-157)
Same 3-way `mv` swap pattern, twice. Same fix.

**Verification after Wave 1**:
- `make test-all` - all pass
- `make bench-sin bench-cos bench-exp bench-log bench-sqrt`
- Expect 3-10 cycles saved per call in functions that route through sigmax;
  fewer for sqrt/exmul/exdiv.

## Wave 2 - Store-then-reload-same-slot -> direct `mv`

No risk, net size reduction, reduces memory traffic through D-cache.

### File: `andes_dpexlog.s` - three sites

Each site has the pattern:
```
sw x10,4(sp) ; sw x11,8(sp) ; sw x12,12(sp) ; sw x13,16(sp) ; ...
mv x10,x18 ; mv x11,x19 ; mv x12,x20
lw x13,4(sp) ; lw x14,8(sp) ; lw x15,12(sp)    # reloads x10,x11,x12 originals
```
The lw block is a move-through-memory of the just-stored values. Replace with
`mv x13,x10; mv x14,x11; mv x15,x12` executed before the `mv x10,x18...`
overwrites. Apply at:
- Lines 130-142 (before the `__gf_exdiv` call)
- Lines 162-170 (before the second `__gf_exmul` call)
- Lines 245-257 (before the `__gf_exadd` call)

Each site: 3 `lw` -> 3 `mv`. Same instruction count, zero memory traffic,
zero load-use stall.

**Verification**: `make test-log bench-log bench-pow bench-logf bench-powf`.

## Wave 3 - Inline `__clzsi2` in dpexlog (follow existing pattern)

### File: `andes_dpexlog.s`
- Delete `.Ldo_clzsi2` at lines 284-303.
- At the two call sites (lines 120 and 195), inline the `__clzsi2` call
  directly following the pattern already used in `sf_fmod.s` /
  `andes_exsub.s` (see commit `5b999a3bd`). The caller can use the already
  live t-registers for `__clzsi2`'s scratch without the 5-register spill.

Each site: removes ~10 instructions of wrapper overhead + 1 call/return pair.
Net code size: shrinks (wrapper body deleted, inline body smaller than the
wrapper because no spill).

**Verification**: `make test-log bench-log bench-pow`; compare `size-*`
artifacts for `andes_dpexlog.o` before/after.

## Wave 4 - Reorder post-call reload blocks to hide load-use

Purely instruction scheduling. No size change.

### Files and sites
- `s_sin.s` lines ~45-52: reloads x10,x11,x12,x13,x14,x15 then
  `addi x20,x12,-1; andi x20,x20,2; beqz x20,.Li4`. Move x12's reload to
  the top (or pair the `addi`/`andi`/`beqz` immediately after `lw x12`'s
  slot is filled but before the unrelated reloads) so the 3-instruction
  dependent chain runs in the shadow of the other loads.
- `andes_dpexexp.s` lines ~76-87: 7 consecutive `lw` from sp then
  `addi x9,x20,-1022` depending on latest load. Reorder x20 reload first.
- `andes_dpexexp.s` lines ~150-160: same pattern with `xor x15,x15,x14`.
- `andes_dpexlog.s` lines ~185-195: 7 reloads then branch on x13 + call
  with x13 arg. Move the x13-dependent sequence before the unrelated loads.
- `andes_dpexlog.s` line 161: `addi x12,x12,1` directly consumes the x12
  just reloaded at line 189. Hoist it or reorder the reload block.

These changes individually save 1-3 cycles per call on in-order cores
with load-to-use latency of 2+.

**Verification**: `make bench-sin bench-cos bench-exp bench-log`.

## Wave 5 - Branchless carry propagation in exadd/exmul

Moderate size impact (small growth) but eliminates hard-to-predict short
branches in hot soft-float helpers. Do this wave only if Waves 1-4
produced less-than-expected cycle improvements, since the size cost is
real (+8-16 bytes per site) and the branches are typically well-predicted
once warm.

### Files (deferred/optional)
- `andes_exadd.s` lines 53-70
- `andes_exmul.s` lines 29-45 and 49-78
- `andes_dpreduct.s` lines 40-108 (five copies of the same idiom)

Replace `add; sltu; beqz; add; sltu; beqz; addi; tail` carry-propagation
trees with straight-line
```
add  x10,x10,x13
sltu c1,x10,x13
add  x11,x11,x14
sltu c2,x11,x14
add  x11,x11,c1
sltu c3,x11,c1
or   carry,c2,c3
```

**Verification**: `make bench-exp bench-log bench-pow bench-sin`. Expect
1-3 cycles per exadd/exmul call on cores with >=2-cycle branch mispredict
penalty.

## Critical files to modify

| File | Wave |
|---|---|
| `libm/machine/riscv/andes_sigmax.s` | 1 |
| `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexsqrt.s` | 1 |

toolUseResult.content:
# Latency Optimization of RISC-V libm Assembly

## Context

The RISC-V soft-float libm assembly in `libm/machine/riscv/` was machine-translated
from Andes v3 (`pushm/popm`, `mulr64`, etc.) into plain RV32IM. The translation
preserved correctness but left behind patterns that hurt per-call latency on
in-order cores:

- Long serial `sw/lw` blocks with load-use hazards feeding immediately-dependent
  arithmetic.
- Values stored to the stack and then re-loaded from the same slot into a
  different register, effectively a `mv` via memory.
- A local `__clzsi2` wrapper (`.Ldo_clzsi2`) that spills 5 registers around every
  call, used on the hot path in `__gf_dpexlog`.
- Three-way register swaps (`mv tmp,a; mv a,b; mv b,tmp`) after multiply blocks
  in `andes_exmul/exdiv/sigmax` that could be removed by renaming later uses.
- At least one fully dead multiply in `andes_sigmax.s`.
- Redundant constant rematerialization (`li x19,32`, `li t6,1`) across branches.

Recent commits (`5b999a3bd`, `14f74476a`) already established the pattern of
inlining `__clzsi2` in `sf_fmod.s` / `andes_exsub` / `sf_modf.s` for exactly this
reason. This plan extends that treatment across the rest of the hot call graph,
targeting latency reduction with <=2% code-size growth per file (some changes
are net-negative size).

Intended outcome: measurable cycle reduction on FPGA benchmarks for `log`,
`logf`, `sin`, `cos`, `exp`, `pow`, `sinh`, `cosh`, `tan`, `asin`, `acos`
(all of which flow through `__gf_dpexlog`, `__gf_sigmax`, `__gf_exmul`, or
`__gf_exadd`), with no accuracy regressions and no significant size growth.

## Scope and Approach

All work is in `libm/machine/riscv/`. Changes are strictly mechanical /
local; no algorithm changes, no new coefficient tables, no interface changes.
Each change is independently measurable via the existing `make bench-<func>`
and `make test-<func>` targets.

The changes are grouped into waves, ordered by `(expected cycle win) / (risk)`.
After each wave: run `make test-all` for correctness and `make bench-<func>`
on the primary impacted functions, then commit via `/commit` skill with the
benchmark deltas captured.

## Wave 1 - Pure dead code / dead moves (free wins, net size reduction)

No risk, no size cost, directly removes instructions from the hot path.

### File: `andes_sigmax.s`
Called from every trig and transcendental (`s_sin`, `s_cos`, `s_exp`, `s_log`,
`s_pow`, etc.) - arguably the single hottest helper in the library.

- Line 53: `mul x14,x10,x18` is immediately overwritten by `mv x14,x20` on
  line 54 with no intervening read of x14. Delete the `mul`.
- Lines 51/54: `mv x20,x14` / `mv x14,x20` pair exists only to preserve x14
  across the (now-deleted) mul. Once the mul is gone, both mvs are dead.
  Remove them and delete the now-unnecessary save of x20.
- Lines 43-47 and 44-46: the 3-way shuffle
  `mv x9,x18; mv x18,x20; mv x15,x12; mv x12,x13; mv x13,t6`
  after the mul block can be collapsed by renaming subsequent uses of x9,
  x12, x13, x18 to read directly from the post-mul registers. Verify by
  tracing live ranges through `.Li2` (line 65).

### File: `andes_dpexsqrt.s`
Two copies of the pattern `mv x21,x14 ; mulhu x15,x11,x15 ; mv x14,x21` around
lines 17-19 and 30-32. `mulhu` does not write x14, so both `mv`s are dead.
Remove them at both sites.

### File: `andes_exmul.s` (lines ~20-22)
`mv t6,x9 ; mv x9,x18 ; mv x18,t6` 3-way swap after the multiply block.
Replace by renaming subsequent uses through `.Lb1`'s fallthrough region. No
instruction to execute, no chain length.

### File: `andes_exdiv.s` (lines ~61-63 and ~155-157)
Same 3-way `mv` swap pattern, twice. Same fix.

**Verification after Wave 1**:
- `make test-all` - all pass
- `make bench-sin bench-cos bench-exp bench-log bench-sqrt`
- Expect 3-10 cycles saved per call in functions that route through sigmax;
  fewer for sqrt/exmul/exdiv.

## Wave 2 - Store-then-reload-same-slot -> direct `mv`

No risk, net size reduction, reduces memory traffic through D-cache.

### File: `andes_dpexlog.s` - three sites

Each site has the pattern:
```
sw x10,4(sp) ; sw x11,8(sp) ; sw x12,12(sp) ; sw x13,16(sp) ; ...
mv x10,x18 ; mv x11,x19 ; mv x12,x20
lw x13,4(sp) ; lw x14,8(sp) ; lw x15,12(sp)    # reloads x10,x11,x12 originals
```
The lw block is a move-through-memory of the just-stored values. Replace with
`mv x13,x10; mv x14,x11; mv x15,x12` executed before the `mv x10,x18...`
overwrites. Apply at:
- Lines 130-142 (before the `__gf_exdiv` call)
- Lines 162-170 (before the second `__gf_exmul` call)
- Lines 245-257 (before the `__gf_exadd` call)

Each site: 3 `lw` -> 3 `mv`. Same instruction count, zero memory traffic,
zero load-use stall.

**Verification**: `make test-log bench-log bench-pow bench-logf bench-powf`.

## Wave 3 - Inline `__clzsi2` in dpexlog (follow existing pattern)

### File: `andes_dpexlog.s`
- Delete `.Ldo_clzsi2` at lines 284-303.
- At the two call sites (lines 120 and 195), inline the `__clzsi2` call
  directly following the pattern already used in `sf_fmod.s` /
  `andes_exsub.s` (see commit `5b999a3bd`). The caller can use the already
  live t-registers for `__clzsi2`'s scratch without the 5-register spill.

Each site: removes ~10 instructions of wrapper overhead + 1 call/return pair.
Net code size: shrinks (wrapper body deleted, inline body smaller than the
wrapper because no spill).

**Verification**: `make test-log bench-log bench-pow`; compare `size-*`
artifacts for `andes_dpexlog.o` before/after.

## Wave 4 - Reorder post-call reload blocks to hide load-use

Purely instruction scheduling. No size change.

### Files and sites
- `s_sin.s` lines ~45-52: reloads x10,x11,x12,x13,x14,x15 then
  `addi x20,x12,-1; andi x20,x20,2; beqz x20,.Li4`. Move x12's reload to
  the top (or pair the `addi`/`andi`/`beqz` immediately after `lw x12`'s
  slot is filled but before the unrelated reloads) so the 3-instruction
  dependent chain runs in the shadow of the other loads.
- `andes_dpexexp.s` lines ~76-87: 7 consecutive `lw` from sp then
  `addi x9,x20,-1022` depending on latest load. Reorder x20 reload first.
- `andes_dpexexp.s` lines ~150-160: same pattern with `xor x15,x15,x14`.
- `andes_dpexlog.s` lines ~185-195: 7 reloads then branch on x13 + call
  with x13 arg. Move the x13-dependent sequence before the unrelated loads.
- `andes_dpexlog.s` line 161: `addi x12,x12,1` directly consumes the x12
  just reloaded at line 189. Hoist it or reorder the reload block.

These changes individually save 1-3 cycles per call on in-order cores
with load-to-use latency of 2+.

**Verification**: `make bench-sin bench-cos bench-exp bench-log`.

## Wave 5 - Branchless carry propagation in exadd/exmul

Moderate size impact (small growth) but eliminates hard-to-predict short
branches in hot soft-float helpers. Do this wave only if Waves 1-4
produced less-than-expected cycle improvements, since the size cost is
real (+8-16 bytes per site) and the branches are typically well-predicted
once warm.

### Files (deferred/optional)
- `andes_exadd.s` lines 53-70
- `andes_exmul.s` lines 29-45 and 49-78
- `andes_dpreduct.s` lines 40-108 (five copies of the same idiom)

Replace `add; sltu; beqz; add; sltu; beqz; addi; tail` carry-propagation
trees with straight-line
```
add  x10,x10,x13
sltu c1,x10,x13
add  x11,x11,x14
sltu c2,x11,x14
add  x11,x11,c1
sltu c3,x11,c1
or   carry,c2,c3
```

**Verification**: `make bench-exp bench-log bench-pow bench-sin`. Expect
1-3 cycles per exadd/exmul call on cores with >=2-cycle branch mispredict
penalty.

## Critical files to modify

|---|---|
| `libm/machine/riscv/andes_sigmax.s` | 1 |
| `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexsqrt.s` | 1 |
| `libm/machine/riscv/andes_exmul.s` | 1 (+5 optional) |

| `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpexexp.s` | 4 |

| `libm/machine/riscv/andes_exadd.s` | 5 (optional) |
| `libm/machine/riscv/andes_dpreduct.s` | 5 (optional) |

- Any coefficient/table changes.

	sw	x15, 24(sp)             # 	swi	$r5, [$sp + #24]
	sw	x9, 28(sp)              # 	swi	$r6, [$sp + #28]
	mv	x13, x10                # 	move	$r3, $r0
	mv	x14, x11                # 	move	$r4, $r1
	mv	x15, x12                # 	move	$r5, $r2
	mv	x10, x18                # 	move	$r0, $r7
	mv	x11, x19                # 	move	$r1, $r8
	mv	x12, x20                # 	move	$r2, $r9
	call	__gf_exadd              # 	jal	__gf_exadd
	mv	x20, x12                # 	move	$r9, $r2
	mv	x19, x11                # 	move	$r8, $r1
	mv	x18, x10                # 	move	$r7, $r0
	lw	x9, 28(sp)              # 	lwi	$r6, [$sp + #28]
	lw	x15, 24(sp)             # 	lwi	$r5, [$sp + #24]
	lw	x14, 20(sp)             # 	lwi	$r4, [$sp + #20]
	lw	x13, 16(sp)             # 	lwi	$r3, [$sp + #16]
	lw	x12, 12(sp)             # 	lwi	$r2, [$sp + #12]
	lw	x11, 8(sp)              # 	lwi	$r1, [$sp + #8]
	lw	x10, 4(sp)              # 	lwi	$r0, [$sp + #4]
.LAret:
	mv	x10, x18                # 	move	$r0, $r7
	mv	x11, x19                # 	move	$r1, $r8
	mv	x12, x20                # 	move	$r2, $r9
.LA999:
	lw	x9, 48(sp)              # 	popm	$r6, $r10
	lw	x18, 52(sp)
	lw	x19, 56(sp)
	lw	x20, 60(sp)
	lw	x21, 64(sp)
	lw	ra, 80(sp)              # 	pop	$lp
	addi	sp, sp, 96
	ret                             # 	ret5	$lp
.Ltmp0:
	.size	__gf_dpexlog, .Ltmp0-__gf_dpexlog
# Local helper: call __clzsi2 with arg in x15, preserves x10-x14, result in x15.
.Ldo_clzsi2:
	addi	sp, sp, -24
	sw	ra, 20(sp)
	sw	x10, 0(sp)
	sw	x11, 4(sp)
	sw	x12, 8(sp)
	sw	x13, 12(sp)
	sw	x14, 16(sp)
	mv	x10, x15
	call	__clzsi2
	mv	x15, x10
	lw	x10, 0(sp)
	lw	x11, 4(sp)
	lw	x12, 8(sp)
	lw	x13, 12(sp)
	lw	x14, 16(sp)
	lw	ra, 20(sp)
	addi	sp, sp, 24
	ret

\ No newline at end of file

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/nick/.claude/skills/commit-optimizer

# Commit Optimizer Skill

Creates well-documented commit messages for optimization work with automatic benchmarking.

## Usage

```
/commit [problem="<description>"] [approach="<description>"]
```

## How It Works

1. **Validates optimization context** — checks that you're in an optimization commit (staged changes present)
2. **Detects what changed** — examines git diff to understand which functions/files were modified
3. **Runs benchmarks** — executes the project's measurement tools (Makefile, commit-size script, or QEMU tests) to capture before/after metrics
4. **Builds commit message** — combines problem statement, approach, and measured results into a structured message

## Commit Message Format

The skill generates messages in this format:

```
optimize: <one-line summary>

Problem:
<explanation of what was slow/large and why it mattered>

Approach:
<description of the optimization technique used>

Result:
<measured improvement with specific metrics>
```

## Examples

**Example 1: Assembly leaf function optimization**
```
optimize: sf_floor.s as leaf function (remove ra push/pop)

Problem:
sf_floor saved and restored return address (ra) unnecessarily, adding 2 instructions (~8 bytes) and extra memory traffic on every call.

Approach:
Eliminated ra push/pop by using tail-call pattern; function now only uses caller-saved registers and doesn't need to preserve ra.

Result:
Code size: 24 → 16 bytes (-33%)
No performance loss (leaf function overhead removed)
```

**Example 2: Register allocation tuning**
```
optimize: sinf.s improve register allocation for FPU operations

Problem:
Register spills were causing extra load/store operations in the hot path of sinf, adding latency on FPU-heavy workloads.

Approach:
Reordered argument reduction to keep intermediate values in registers longer; used callee-saved registers more strategically to reduce spill pressure.

Result:
Code size: 312 → 298 bytes (-4%)
Execution time: 142 → 136 cycles (-4%)
```

## What the Skill Does

- **Auto-detects function** from changed files (e.g., `sf_floor.s` → optimizing `sf_floor`)
- **Runs project benchmarks** to measure size/performance before/after
- **Extracts metrics** from benchmark output (code size, cycle count, etc.)
- **Allows customization** — you can provide problem and approach strings to override auto-detection

## When NOT to Use

This skill is **only** for optimization commits. Use regular git commit for:
- Bug fixes (unless they're performance-related)
- Refactoring without size/performance gains
- Documentation changes
- Adding new features

## Required Parameters (optional)

- `problem` — Brief explanation of what was suboptimal (auto-detected from function changes if omitted)
- `approach` — Description of the optimization technique (auto-detected from git diff if omitted)

The benchmark result is **always** captured automatically.

## Integration with Your Project

This skill is designed for Picolibc RISC-V libm work:
- Looks for Makefile `compare` target to run benchmarks
- Falls back to `commit-size` script if available
- Parses QEMU output or size tool output to extract metrics
- Works with both `-s` assembly files and C implementations in `hardfloat/`

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
optimize: eliminate dead code and memory round-trips in hot helpers

Problem:
Three latency issues in frequently-called soft-float helpers:

1. andes_sigmax.s (called by every trig and transcendental): a `mul`
   result was immediately overwritten by a `mv`, making it dead.  Two
   surrounding `mv`s existed solely to preserve x14 across the dead
   multiply -- those were dead too.  Net: 3 wasted instructions per
   loop iteration in the hottest helper in the library.

2. andes_dpexsqrt.s: two identical patterns where x14 was saved into
   x21 around a `mulhu` that does not write x14, then restored.  The
   save/restore pair was completely unnecessary.

3. andes_dpexlog.s: three sites that stored x10/x11/x12 to stack and
   immediately reloaded them into x13/x14/x15 via lw instead of mv
   (store-to-load forwarding through memory).  Also, a 19-instruction
   `.Ldo_clzsi2` wrapper spilled 5 registers around every call to
   __clzsi2 -- called twice per log invocation on the normalization
   path.

Approach:
- sigmax: delete dead `mul x14` and the two `mv`s that bracketed it.
- dpexsqrt: remove the two `mv x21,x14` / `mv x14,x21` pairs.
- dpexlog site 1 (pre-exdiv): eliminate the 3 stores to slots 4/8/12
  (never reloaded after exdiv) and replace the 3 loads with direct mv.
- dpexlog sites 2+3 (pre-exmul, pre-exadd): replace the 3 lw with mv;
  stores are kept because the slots are reloaded after those calls.
- dpexlog: inline __clzsi2 (binary-search CLZ, pattern from sf_modf.s)
  at both call sites using t6/x17 as scratch; delete .Ldo_clzsi2.

Result (FPGA cycle counts, 100-iteration bench):
  log:    601078 -> 549431  (-8.6%)
  log10:  652446 -> 603253  (-7.5%)
  pow:    962158 -> 885426  (-8.0%)
  sinh:   640016 -> 605347  (-5.4%)
  acos:   157677 -> 143129  (-9.2%)
  atan:   627806 -> 609696  (-2.9%)
  tanh:   668064 -> 657355  (-1.6%)
  cosh:   641680 -> 632475  (-1.4%)
  tan:    714510 -> 704671  (-1.4%)
  asin:   151494 -> 149652  (-1.2%)
  No regressions vs bench_all_2.txt baseline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Committed. Summary of what was done:

**Wave 1 - Dead code (andes_sigmax.s, andes_dpexsqrt.s):**
- `sigmax`: removed dead `mul x14,x10,x18` + 2 surrounding `mv`s that only existed to preserve x14 across it. Saves 3 instructions per loop iteration in the hottest helper.
- `dpexsqrt`: removed 2 pairs of `mv x21,x14` / `mv x14,x21` around `mulhu` instructions that don't touch x14.

**Wave 2 - Memory round-trips eliminated (andes_dpexlog.s):**
- 3 sites that stored x10/x11/x12 to stack then immediately loaded them back into x13/x14/x15 via `lw`. Replaced with direct `mv` (or eliminated the stores entirely where the slots were never reloaded).

**Wave 3 - Inline CLZ (andes_dpexlog.s):**
- Deleted the 19-instruction `.Ldo_clzsi2` wrapper that spilled 5 registers around `__clzsi2`. Inlined the binary-search CLZ at both call sites using t-registers as scratch (same pattern established in `sf_modf.s`).

**Results vs baseline:** log -8.6%, log10 -7.5%, pow -8.0%, sinh -5.4%, acos -9.2%, atan -2.9%, with no regressions.

input.command:
git add andes_fpexlog.s && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
optimize: eliminate dead code and inline CLZ in __gf_fpexlog

Problem:
Same two issues as __gf_dpexlog (fixed in previous commit), now in
the single-precision path used by logf, log10f, and powf:

1. Dead multiply: `mul x12, x10, x10` at the self-square step was
   immediately overwritten by `mv x12, x21`, along with the
   surrounding save/restore of x12 that existed solely to protect
   x12 across the now-dead multiply.

2. `.Ldo_clzsi2_f` wrapper spilled 6 registers (x10-x15) around
   every call to __clzsi2.  Called twice per __gf_fpexlog invocation
   (normalization at function entry + optional second normalization
   on the subnormal path), this added ~30 instructions of pure
   overhead per log call.

Approach:
- Delete `mv x21,x12`, `mul x12,x10,x10`, `mv x12,x21` (3 insns).
- Inline CLZ at both call sites using t6/x17 as scratch, same
  binary-search pattern as sf_modf.s and __gf_dpexlog.  Delete
  .Ldo_clzsi2_f.

Result (FPGA cycle counts, 100-iteration bench vs bench_all_2.txt):
  logf:    234947 -> 194690  (-17.1%)
  log10f:  270496 -> 209978  (-22.4%)  [was 0.95x, now 1.23x vs ref]
  powf:    351942 -> 311355  (-11.5%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
`andes_fpexlog.s` had the same patterns as `andes_dpexlog.s`. Three changes:

1. **Dead mul** at the self-square step - `mul x12,x10,x10` result immediately overwritten by `mv x12,x21`, plus the surrounding save/restore of x12. Deleted 3 instructions.

2. **Inline CLZ** at both call sites - `.Ldo_clzsi2_f` spilled 6 registers (~30 instructions overhead) per call. Now inlined using t6/x17 as scratch. Deleted the wrapper.

Results vs baseline:
- `logf`: -17.1%
- `log10f`: -22.4% (was 0.95x worse than ref, now 1.23x better)
- `powf`: -11.5%
